title: Pumpkin Fritters – that soak up the syrup
excerpt: My mother always made these pumpkin fritters for us out of Wenresepte. It felt like you were allowed to eat pudding and food at the same time. My mother has now been gone for a good while and I miss her today. So I'm making these fritters in honour of my mother, Sarietha Uys. It's soooooooo delicious.

seo.title: Pumpkin Fritters - that soak up the syrup - Marinda Kook

html: My mother always made these pumpkin fritters for us out of Wenresepte.  It felt like you were allowed to eat pudding and food at the same time.  My mother has now been gone for a good while and I miss her today.  So I'm making these fritters in honour of my mother, Sarietha Uys. It's soooooooo delicious.

recipe.title: Pumpkin Fritters – that soak up the syrup
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

ingredientGroups.title: Fritter batter
items: 500 ml oil for frying the fritters

steps: Beat the cooked, mashed pumpkin, eggs and salt together.
Mix in the baking powder and self-raising flour.
Heat the oil in a pan and spoon spoonfuls of the batter into the hot oil.
Turn over when browned and drain on kitchen paper in a bowl.
Keep it warm.
Now make the syrup. I made my syrup in the microwave, but you can also make it in a pot on the stove.
Microwave the sugar, milk, water and butter for 5 minutes. Stir until the sugar has dissolved.
Mix the cornflour with a little bit of water until smooth.
Stir it into the sugar mixture and microwave for another 2 minutes.
Pour the warm syrup over the warm fritters.

notes: This is one of the tastiest dishes ever recorded in our food history. You might just sit and eat the whole bowl by yourself…
